schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes. NAME PROVISIONAL: `host-seam-installer` is CC-picked and NOT yet Akien-ratified (contrast cairn-command / intentions-model-compiler, whose names were settled at the signature gate in-conversation). The name says what it projects over (host-seams); the CLI face would be `cairn install`. Flag for Akien's ratify before it hardens.
intention: THE INSTALLER as a PROJECTION over host-seams: a machine makes ITSELF ready by walking its own host-seams — offering each one's declared {applies-to filter, apply, verify}, gated by the operator's yes/no — so setup is never hand-wired step by step. Answer Y/N down a compiled list and the box configures itself. The list is COMPILED from what host-seams already declare (they ride in intentions/_model.json), never hand-maintained. Each entry runs a small pipeline: filter (relevant to THIS host?) -> ask_user (want it?) -> apply (run the recipe) -> verify (confirm it took, or say it didn't, loud). Idempotent by construction — verify-first, so an already-applied seam is confirmed-and-skipped, not blindly re-run. The gate is a DUMB `ask_user.sh <question> <type> <on-yes> <on-no>` that owns nothing it runs.
why: The other half of the bare-machine SEED CLAIM (CLAUDE.md roots table: carry `intentions/` to a bare machine and regrow the system). The intentions-model-compiler regrows the CODE; this readies the MACHINE — and the compiler even supplies the source list (it embeds each host-seam's apply/verify content into _model.json). Founding intention: get Akien out of running the system — the box sets itself up, nobody remembering which hooks to wire. Law 1: the setup list is COMPILED from what host-seams already declare, never re-typed — a hand-listed installer re-derives the settled (the exact defect it exists to kill). Law 4: host-seams are apply/verify recipes with expiring seals; this is the RUNNER that machinery was waiting for. Kin to intentions-model-compiler (a projection over a corpus), cross-referenced NOT merged: that projects sources->model, this applies host-seams->a ready machine — distinct owner, distinct job, not exclusive-and-terminal.
traces_to: the roots table / seed claim (CLAUDE.md — carry `intentions/` to a bare machine and regrow the system; the installer is the HOST-SEAM half of that, the compiler the code half)
founding intention (get Akien out of running the system — a machine that sets itself up, no hand-wiring)
Law 1 (the setup list is compiled from what host-seams already declare, not re-typed — re-listing them by hand is the defect)
Law 4 (host-seams: implementation lives where git can't see, carried as a replayable apply + a re-runnable verify with an expiring seal — the installer is their runner)
Law 6 (ask_user.sh is a dumb gate that owns nothing it runs — resolve-and-hand-off, like the cairn dispatcher)
kinship: intentions-model-compiler (projection over a corpus — same pattern, different corpus/owner; cross-referenced not merged, and it FEEDS this: host-seam recipes ride in _model.json)
cross-ref: sudo-relay (a privileged seam's apply — /etc/profile.d, systemd units — routes through the sudo-relay, not ambient root)
state: code-seam@v1: [THINKME] -> TICKETME -> BUILDME -> PROVEME -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (CAST 2026-07-23 via /sorted; cursor at TICKETME. Completeness gate ran and was NOT red — it surfaced two named dependencies (below) and one recorded distinction, which is deconstruction, not a defect. NOTHING BUILT YET. The key structural finding: host-seams today declare apply/verify as PROSE (cairn-command's PATH seam, intentions_model_compiler child c's hook seam) and have NO `applies-to` field — so the projection needs a uniform machine-readable HOST-SEAM SCHEMA to gather them, which likely needs its own cast (a change spanning many charters + the compiler). Child a (ask_user.sh) and child c (the `cairn install` CLI face) do NOT wait on that schema — buildable now; child b (the projection engine) does.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — a proof a hollow build could not pass: give the installer a known set of host-seams (some applies-to-this-host true, some false; some already-applied, some not) and assert it OFFERS exactly the applicable ones, APPLIES exactly the yes-answered ones, VERIFIES each apply took (a seam whose verify fails is reported RED, never silently 'installed'), SKIPS the already-applied (idempotent, verify-first), and NEVER hand-lists (the offered set is derived from the host-seam corpus, so a seam added to a charter appears with no installer edit). ask_user.sh (child a) proves separately: it forks to the right branch on Y/N, owns no logic of the branches, fails loud on bad input (CP1). VALIDATION persisted beside-code via cairn/tester/validation_store.persist_validation. Invariant per code-seam: proved + feeds back to origin on failure (Laws 3+8).
falsifier: a fresh OR drifted machine, handed `intentions/` + the installer, reaches a fully-applied, VERIFIED state with the operator only answering Y/N — and re-running is idempotent. Wrong-intent signals: (1) it HAND-LISTS steps instead of compiling them from host-seams (re-deriving the settled — the Law 1 breach it exists to kill); (2) ask_user.sh grows knowledge of what a step DOES (stops being a dumb gate — Law 6); (3) it applies WITHOUT verifying (an 'installed' seam that never took — Law 3, an unmeasured claim passing for done); (4) it becomes a SECOND record of host-seam truth instead of reading the charters (the charter is the source; the installer must read, not copy); (5) it SILENTLY skips a seam that failed its filter or apply rather than reporting the gap (Law 7 — a setup hole must be loud).
horizon: first real fresh-clone / bare-machine install that applies + verifies the KNOWN host-seams (the cairn PATH wiring, the recompile-gate FileChanged hook, the usage-widget hook) from Y/N answers. Re-check THEN: (a) the /challenge question raised at birth — is the installer's APPLY the same act as the recompile-gate's POKE, i.e. do host-seams want a COMMON RUNNER (apply / verify / emit) that BOTH the installer and the live gates call? If so, factor it out — but not before this second consumer proves the seam; (b) does an UNATTENDED install need a defaults/answer-file (a profile) so it is not strictly human-gated — reconciling the interactive Y/N with the get-Akien-out-of-the-loop aim; (c) whether the host-seam schema (the waits_on) wants to be this ticket's child or its own spanning node.
children: (buildable now, cairn — no deps) a — ask_user.sh: the DUMB gate. `ask_user.sh <question> <type> <on-yes> <on-no>` — asks, and on the answer runs one of two command strings via `bash -c` (each branch quoted as ONE arg, so a multi-token branch like `echo 'Not selected'` is unambiguous). The `<type>` slot (YN first) leaves room for a menu / path-prompt without changing the shape. Owns NOTHING it runs (Law 6); loud on bad input (CP1). The UU 'resolve and hand off' pattern with a Y/N fork.

(waits on the host-seam schema) b — the PROJECTION ENGINE: gather every host-seam's {applies-to, apply, verify} from the corpus (intentions/_model.json / the charters), and per seam run filter -> ask_user -> apply -> verify, idempotent (verify-first). The engine that makes the machine ready; berths in cairn/ beside its code; this ticket migrates there to become its history when it proves.

(the CLI face, waits on cairn-command — already landed) c — the `cairn install` verb: a `bin/cmd/install` that pokes the installer from the command line — the hand-crank trigger, a thin registration against the cairn dispatcher (the same shape as `cairn compile`).
waits_on: THE HOST-SEAM SCHEMA is the load-bearing dependency: for the projection to gather {applies-to, apply, verify} uniformly, host-seams must declare them in a machine-readable shape (today they are PROSE, and `applies-to` does not exist). This likely needs ITS OWN cast — it spans many charters (every host_seam block), the compiler (to surface the field), and a schema definition; possibly a spanning intention. Child b waits on it. Child a (ask_user.sh) and child c (the `cairn install` face) DO NOT — buildable now. NOT a wait, cross-referenced: privileged seams' apply routes through the sudo-relay; the intentions-model-compiler already embeds the recipes this consumes.
provenance: Cast 2026-07-23 via /sorted, in the design conversation that followed building intentions_model_compiler child c (the recompile-gate host-seam) and the mid-2026 CC-primitives sweep (the research answering 'what am I missing about how to use you best' — Cairn ahead on philosophy, the gap being operational primitives mounted behind Cairn's doors). Born from Akien's sketch of an installer as a series of ask_user callbacks (`ask_user.sh "do you want the usage display hook?" YN <on-yes> <on-no>`); CC critiqued the command line (multi-token branches need quoting, keep ask_user dumb) and ELEVATED it to a PROJECTION over host-seams — Law 1: compile the setup list from what host-seams already declare, don't hand-list. The completeness gate surfaced the host-seam-schema dependency and recorded the autonomy DISTINCTION (a bootstrap install where the operator chooses is not the same as operating the system — not a collision with get-Akien-out-of-the-loop). Name is CC-picked, provisional, pending Akien's ratify.
